title: Write a Kubernetes Audit Policy
description:
## Kubernetes Audit Policy

Audit logging lets you track who did what on the cluster. The kube-apiserver reads an **audit policy file** to decide which events to log and at which verbosity level.

**Your task:**

Write an audit policy file at `/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml` with the following rules (in order):

1. Log all operations on `secrets` in any namespace at level **`RequestResponse`**.
2. Log read operations (`get`, `list`, `watch`) on `pods` at level **`Metadata`**.
3. **Drop** (level `None`) all events for the `system:masters` group.
4. Log everything else at level **`Metadata`** as a catch-all.

```bash
# Verify the policy file exists and contains the key fields:
cat /etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml
```

hints.title: Audit policy structure
hints.body: An audit policy is a YAML file with `apiVersion: audit.k8s.io/v1`, `kind: Policy`, and a `rules:` list. Order matters — first matching rule wins.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF > /etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml
apiVersion: audit.k8s.io/v1
kind: Policy
rules:
- level: RequestResponse
  resources:
  - group: ""
    resources: ["secrets"]
- level: Metadata
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  resources:
  - group: ""
    resources: ["pods"]
- level: None
  userGroups: ["system:masters"]
- level: Metadata
EOF

commands.description: Audit policy file is valid YAML with kind: Policy
Rule 1: level is RequestResponse and targets secrets
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; p=yaml.safe_load(open('/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml')); r=p['rules'][0]; print('ok' if r['level']=='RequestResponse' and any('secrets' in x.get('resources',[]) for x in r.get('resources',[])) else 'fail')"

commands.description: Rule 2: level is Metadata, targets pods, verbs include get/list/watch
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; p=yaml.safe_load(open('/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml')); print('ok' if any(r.get('level')=='Metadata' and any('pods' in x.get('resources',[]) for x in r.get('resources',[])) and set(r.get('verbs',[])) >= {'get','list','watch'} for r in p['rules']) else 'fail')"

commands.description: Rule 3: level is None for system:masters group
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; p=yaml.safe_load(open('/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml')); print('ok' if any(r.get('level')=='None' and 'system:masters' in r.get('userGroups',[]) for r in p['rules']) else 'fail')"

commands.description: Rule 4: catch-all is level Metadata with no other selectors
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; p=yaml.safe_load(open('/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml')); last=p['rules'][-1]; print('ok' if set(last.keys())=={'level'} and last['level']=='Metadata' else 'fail')"
